km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw於1563年出生在英國。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1592年享年29歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw于1563年出生在英国。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1592年享年29岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moreton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (West Midlands): variant of   Morton   1.
Spanish (Moretón): nickname from moretón ‘brown, tanned’ (of skin).
French: nickname from a double diminutive of   More   2 ‘Moor’. Compare   Moret  .

variants.name: Morton
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of the many places called Mor(e)ton, named with Old English mōr ‘moor’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. There has probably been some confusion with Morten , and perhaps also with Murton . This English name has also been established in Ireland since the 13th century.
American shortened and altered form of Swedish Mårtensson or Mortensson (see Martenson and Mortenson ), which could also be substituted for the Finnish cognate Marttinen.
French: shortened form of   Moreton   3.

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Corbett  .

History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Bradshaw
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Bradshaw in Lancashire, Yorkshire, and Derbyshire, from Old English brād ‘broad’ + sceaga ‘thicket’.

variants.name: Brayshaw
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Bradshaw  , reflecting the local Yorkshire pronunciation.

nameMeanings.name: Rondle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English roundel (Old French rondel), a diminutive of round(e) ‘rotund, plump’. Compare Round .
English: perhaps from the personal name Rondulf, a West Midlands variant of Randulf. See Randolph and Randall .
English: habitational name from Randall Wood in Shorne (Kent), from Old French rondel ‘circle, something circular’.

variants.name: Rundel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hermann.
German: from Ru(o)nelin, a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on rūn ‘secret, rune, cryptogram’.
English: nickname from Old French rondel, a diminutive of round(e) ‘rotund, plump’. Compare Rundle .

variants.name: Round
meanings: English (Worcestershire and Staffordshire): nickname for a plump person, from Middle English round(e) (Old French rond) ‘rotund, plump’ (from Latin rotundus).

variants.name: Rundall
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Runnells
meanings: English (Cornwall): variant of Rundle , with post-medieval excrescent -s and loss of medial -d-. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Rundell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Rundle  .
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element rund ‘round’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.
Americanized form of German   Rundel  .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw was born in 1563, in England. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1592, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw est né en 1563, à Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1592, à l’âge de 29 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw wurde 1563 in England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1592 im Alter von 29 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw è nato il nel 1563 nel England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1592, all'età di 29 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshawは1563年にイギリスで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1592年に29歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw(은)는 1563년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1592년 29세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw nasceu em 1563, em Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1592, com 29 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw родился в 1563 г. в местности Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1592 г. в возрасте 29 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Rondle Moreton Corbet Bradshaw nació en 1563, en Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1592, a la edad de 29 años.
